description: Magic that summons, creates, and transports — calling creatures from nothing, fabricating objects, opening portals, and traversing planes.
abilities: Blink
Vanishing in a gentle gust of wind and reappearing instantly at a chosen nearby location.
Chaos Ball

Summoning a tiny elastic ball that, once thrown, bounces off everything it touches while avoiding creatures, accelerating with each impact until it escapes into the atmosphere after a brief and glorious rampage. In its wake, pottery shatters, glass breaks, light furniture topples, and most other fragile things in the area meet an unfortunate end.
Conjure

Willing an imagined object into existence at a nearby location. The object can be anything nonmagical that is no larger than an elephant. Success depends on the clarity of your visualization — a perfectly conceived image produces the object flawlessly, a close but imperfect vision yields something with a defect, and a badly muddled attempt produces a random misshapen item instead.
Create

Tapping raw magic to convert its energy into a single, nonmagical, inanimate object that fits within a modest space. The object is crafted perfectly and is real in every sense — precious gems, flawless weapons, exact replicas. However, the spell is extraordinarily taxing; each successive casting demands more effort than the last.
Dark Door

Knocking on any door to transform it into a temporary portal to a known shadow plane. You must be aware of the plane's existence — for instance, recognizing the pocket dimension within a magical container. The door reveals the plane's interior, and you and your allies may enter freely. If the door closes, the portal vanishes; anyone inside must find another way out.
Eternity Gate

Projecting past all realities to glimpse a place outside time and space, posing a single question to eternity itself and seeking a factual truth. At full success, an answer is received along with a follow-up question. A strong result yields a clear answer. A partial success returns the answer but traps the caster's body for a week while their mind ages a year. Failure means a week of absence with no answer and ten years of mental aging, leaving the mind wracked and scarred. A catastrophic failure traps the mind for what feels like an incomprehensible exile, and upon returning after a week, the caster's personality is fundamentally changed: old ideals, flaws, and dreams wash away, replaced by new ones forged in the ordeal.
Familiar

Summoning a tiny spectral creature — a bird, lizard, butterfly, or anything of comparable size — as the avatar of a random spirit from your world. The familiar becomes your ally, following instructions and carrying objects no larger than a coin purse and no heavier than a few pounds, though it cannot attack. It has its own personality and background, and may choose to leave if mistreated. The familiar is invulnerable to non-magical harm but fragile against magic; if destroyed, the bond is broken. Only one familiar can be maintained at a time.
Force Field

Conjuring a paper-thin wall of force in any shape that prevents nonmagical creatures and objects from passing through. The field can block passage on one or both sides, remain stationary or move telepathically — serving as a barrier, a bridge, or an invisible platform beneath your feet. It spans a wide area but can be shattered by sufficient force.
Gate

Binding yourself to a room you or an ally owns, establishing it as your Circle — your single anchored destination. At any time thereafter, a snap of the fingers teleports all willing nearby allies directly to your Circle.
Helen's Fantastic Feast

Conjuring an elaborate feast of any culinary style, complete with food, drink, furniture, dinnerware, and table decorations, enough to feed a large gathering. Consuming the meal restores a moderate amount of vitality.
Invoke

Leaving the worldly body behind in a quiet, solitary place and creating an astral projection that enters a liminal plane of existence, experienced as a dreamworld in the stars: perhaps a lush paradise in a nebula, an idyllic homestead, or a temple in the fires of creation. There, the invoker meets with an avatar of their deity or, if unaligned, an avatar of supernatural wisdom. A brief audience is granted in which questions may be posed and truthful answers received, such as whether one is on the right path, whether someone is who they claim to be, whether one is living up to their ideal, whether one's actions have unknowingly caused harm, or how one might seek redemption. While in the trance, only a vague sense of danger around the mortal body remains.
Peerless Pilot

Conjuring a fantastic hot air balloon from another dimension, large enough to carry the caster and their companions. The caster must be outdoors in a clearing with room for the balloon to form. The balloon's appearance is entirely up to the caster, and it is piloted by a small, talkative illusory creature that takes instructions and remembers the caster and their past journeys if summoned again. Powered by a flaming sphere above the carriage, the balloon can soar as high as the tallest mountain peaks and travel at a brisk pace. Both the carriage and balloon can sustain a fair amount of damage before being destroyed. The balloon may be dismissed at any time, vanishing with its pilot and returning home.
Pegasus Cloak

Bestowing illusory feather cloaks upon any number of nearby creatures for a short duration. Cloaked creatures hover just above the ground and move by gliding, drifting gently downward whenever they descend rather than falling.
Planecraft

Conjuring a shadow plane filled with anything you can imagine except sentient beings. Everything within is ultimately illusory and cannot leave the plane. At its most modest, the plane is no larger than a mansion. With greater power it expands to the size of a village, then to a plane with no size limit at all. At its most dangerous extreme, all safeguards are removed and the illusions within become capable of causing real harm — to others and to you.
Portal

Conjuring a crackling, sparking portal of any shape and orientation, no larger than a door, at a chosen nearby location. The portal looks like a window to whatever lies on the other side. Casting the spell again connects the new portal to the previous one, and both remain open until you choose to close them. Anyone that fits may pass through.
Rift

Summoning a magnificent gateway that opens a portal to the planar nexus of your current universe. Each universe's gateway is unique — a stone archway, an iron gate, a humble wooden door. The portal is extraordinarily difficult to sustain and remains open only briefly. Any creature or object that fits may pass through, but while the gateway can be summoned from anywhere, the exit point in the nexus is always the same.
Shadow Haven

Placing a hand on a dwelling and instantly duplicating its interior inside a shadow plane. The duplication looks and feels real but is illusory; nothing can be taken from it. The dwelling can range from a small shed to a grand castle. The physical entrance in the real world doubles as a secret portal to the haven, and the caster may grant or revoke access to anyone at any time. A creature with permission who knows of the haven may choose to step through the portal by willing it; otherwise the door functions normally. The haven persists permanently until dispelled, dismissed, or the spell is recast, at which point any creatures inside are instantly expelled through the portal.
Shield

Summoning a magical shield that appears as an aura of soft light around the body, capable of absorbing a modest amount of damage before shattering. Any force dealt in excess of the shield's capacity passes through and strikes the caster directly. The shield persists until it absorbs a blow that meets or exceeds its threshold.
Telekinesis

Moving a nearby creature or object using only your mind. A gentle application allows controlled manipulation for several seconds, while a forceful throw hurls the target a short distance and deals minor damage. Additional willpower extends the throwing range and increases the damage proportionally — the more effort invested, the farther and harder the target is flung.
Teleport

Snapping your fingers to teleport yourself and any willing nearby creatures to any chosen destination, leaving behind a small shockwave of displaced air. A flawless teleport delivers everyone safely and effortlessly. More commonly the arrival is uneventful but draining. Turbulence may cause injury or destroy a magical possession. In worse cases, some travelers are scattered to unknown locations. A catastrophic failure tears the magic apart in an arcane explosion, injuring allies and potentially killing other travelers.
